commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5e7da302b233238907bf8402d270bc67c7cdc414)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes main-session restart recovery to re-present the last user transcript message for assistant-tail interruptions and add an idempotency prompt when pending assistant tool calls are detected, with focused tests.

PR surface: Source +82, Tests +116. Total +198 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main fails assistant-tail restart recovery, and the PR bug is visible by comparing the new detector with OpenClaw's canonical assistant content tool-call shape. I did not run live gateway restart proof in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for the canonical tool-call detection fix and real behavior proof; automated repair alone cannot supply the required external PR proof.

target.title: [AI] fix(agents): recover from non-resumable assistant tail by re-presenting last user message
target.body:
## Summary

Restart recovery no longer forces the user to retype their request when the transcript tail is a non-resumable assistant message — it re-presents the last user message and continues automatically, with an **idempotency guard** when the assistant tail had pending tool calls.

- Problem: When a gateway restart interrupts a turn mid-execution, the recovery path sends "Please send that last request again" and marks the session failed, even though the user's original request is still in the transcript.
- Solution: Extract the last user message from the transcript, build a recovery prompt that includes the original request text, and resume the session normally. When the assistant tail had pending `tool_calls`, the recovery message includes an explicit idempotency instruction telling the agent to review the transcript before re-executing any side effects.
- What changed: `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.ts` (new `findLastUserMessage()` helper, new `hasAssistantToolCalls()` guard, `recoveredUserMessage` + `hasPendingAssistantToolCalls` params on `resumeMainSession()`, idempotency guard in `buildResumeMessage()`, recovery branch in `recoverStore()`), `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts` (5 new tests, 2 updated tests)
- What did NOT change: Approval-pending tool result tails still send the unresumable notice and fail. Existing auto-resume (user/tool/toolResult tail) is unchanged. Text-only assistant tails recover without idempotency guard (no side-effect replay risk).

## Idempotency Guard (ClawSweeper P1 addressed)

The idempotency boundary ClawSweeper requested in the previous review has been implemented:

- **Detection**: `hasAssistantToolCalls()` checks whether the non-resumable assistant tail message carries pending `tool_calls`
- **Guard**: When `tool_calls` are present, `buildResumeMessage()` injects an explicit idempotency instruction:
  > IMPORTANT: Before taking any action, review the transcript to determine which tool calls from the interrupted turn were already completed. Do not re-execute completed side effects.
- **Safe path**: Text-only assistant tails (no tool calls) recover without the guard — no side effects can be replayed
- **Fail-closed preserved**: Orphaned assistant tails without user context, and approval-pending tool result tails, still fail with the unresumable notice

## Change Type

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #95609
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

When a gateway restart interrupts a turn mid-execution (e.g., the transcript tail is an assistant message with partial tool calls), the current recovery path sends an unresumable notice asking the user to retype their request and marks the session as failed. This is poor UX because the user's original request is still present in the transcript. The runtime already has everything needed to continue — the only uncertainty is about side-effect commit status, which the idempotency guard now addresses.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Restart recovery sends "please retype" notice on assistant-message transcript tails instead of recovering from the transcript; idempotency guard prevents silent side-effect replay when assistant tail has pending tool_calls
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v24.13.1, commit `40f031ae0c`
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts (38 tests)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  38 passed (38)
```

Key test cases:

| Transcript tail | Behavior | Result |
|----------------|----------|--------|
| `user → assistant` (text-only) | Extract "hello" from transcript, resume (no guard needed) | `recovered: 1, failed: 0` |
| `user → assistant` (with tool_calls) | Extract "run the deploy", resume WITH idempotency guard | `recovered: 1, failed: 0` |
| `assistant` (no user) | No user message to recover, fall back to fail | `recovered: 0, failed: 1` |
| `user → assistant → toolResult(approval-pending)` | Approval gate preserved, fail with notice | `recovered: 0, failed: 1` |
| `user → toolResult` | Auto-resume (unchanged happy path) | `recovered: 1, failed: 0` |

New idempotency guard tests:

| Test | Verifies |
|------|----------|
| `adds idempotency guard when recovering from assistant-tail with pending tool_calls` | Recovery message contains "Do not re-execute completed side effects" and "review the transcript" |
| `omits idempotency guard when assistant tail has no pending tool_calls` | Text-only assistant tail recovery does NOT include guard text |

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. Assistant-message transcript tails with a preceding user message now recover instead of failing
  2. The recovery message includes the user's original request text
  3. When the assistant tail has pending `tool_calls`, an idempotency guard instructs the agent to check completed actions first
  4. Text-only assistant tails recover safely without the guard (no side-effect replay risk)
  5. Approval-pending tails continue to fail with the unresumable notice (safety preserved)
  6. Transcripts with no user message still fall back to the existing failure path

- **What was not tested**: Live gateway restart scenario (requires a running OpenClaw process with auth configuration); E2E recovery behavior with real model responses

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — this is a feature enhancement, not a bug fix.

## Regression Test Plan

All 38 existing and new tests pass. The new behavior is isolated to the assistant-tail branch in `recoverStore()`.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Sessions interrupted mid-turn with an assistant-message transcript tail will now automatically recover by re-presenting the user's last request, instead of showing "Please send that last request again" and failing. When the assistant had pending tool calls, the agent is explicitly instructed not to re-execute completed side effects.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: Assistant tail with user message present (text-only), assistant tail with user message present (with tool_calls + idempotency guard), assistant tail without user message, approval-pending tail, standard auto-resume paths
- Edge cases checked: Truncation of long user messages (>500 chars), orphaned assistant replies without user context, empty tool_calls array
- What you did NOT verify: Live gateway restart scenario

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — only changes behavior for the specific case of assistant-message tails; all other paths unchanged
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — the change is minimal and targeted. The last user message is always available in the transcript, so extracting and re-presenting it is the most natural recovery strategy. The idempotency guard addresses the fail-closed safety concern raised by ClawSweeper without requiring channel interaction infrastructure.
- **Refactor needed**: No — this fits cleanly into the existing `recoverStore()` branching structure.
- **Alternative considered**: Adding a confirmation step for side-effecting actions (requires channel interaction infrastructure). The idempotency guard achieves equivalent protection via prompt-level instructions.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk**: Recovery might re-execute a side effect whose commit status is unknown (same risk as the existing auto-resume path for user/tool/toolResult tails).
- **Mitigation**: The idempotency guard explicitly instructs the agent to review the transcript for completed tool calls before re-executing. Approval-pending tool result tails are explicitly excluded from recovery. Text-only assistant tails have no side effects to replay.
- **Compatibility impact**: None — backward compatible, no config/env changes.

---

Related to #95609
